name: Stuffed Pork Chops
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor or blender, process the apples, 4 tablespoons of butter, sugar, vanilla, and nutmeg until smooth. Set aside.
2. In a small bowl, thoroughly combine all seasoning mix ingredients. Set aside.
3. Prepare the pork chops by cutting a large pocket into the larger side of each chop to hold the stuffing.
4. In a large skillet, brown the ground pork in the remaining butter over high heat for about 3 minutes. Add onions, bell peppers, garlic, and 2 tablespoons of the seasoning mix, stirring well. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally and scraping pan bottom. Stir in green chilies and their juice, continuing to cook until mixture is well browned (about 6-8 minutes). Add stock and cook 5 minutes, stirring frequently. Stir in bread crumbs and cook about 2 more minutes, stirring constantly.
5. Remove from heat. Sprinkle the remaining seasoning mix evenly on both sides of chops and inside pockets, pressing by hand.
6. Arrange chops with pocket side up in an ungreased baking pan. Spoon about 1/4 cup stuffing into each pocket; reserve remaining stuffing. Bake at 400°F until meat is done (about 1 hour 10 minutes).
7. Place the remaining stuffing in a small pan and reheat for the last 20 minutes.
8. Serve immediately with chops arranged on top of portion of remaining stuffing.
